description:
My PORTUGUESE FOOD Playlist 👉  https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL3vWM3Uc1MgZVL6dn_TVgRIqMJlvJsufZ

Lisbon has its own version of street food — quieter, more local, and deeply rooted in Portuguese everyday life.
In this video, I explore eight Portuguese street foods that define the city’s food culture, not through fancy plates, but through cafés, bakeries, and small tascas where locals actually eat.

This videos features my journey thorough:
- A bifana in a corner bar where the morning starts slow. 
- The original Pastéis de Belém, linked to a centuries-old Portuguese recipe. 
- A Bola de Berlim with a surprising history.
- A plate of piri-piri chicken in a tiny grill house where the whole experience feels alive. 
- Fresh pão com chouriço baked through the night. 
- A codfish croquette filled with Serra da Estrela cheese. 
- Simple seafood petiscos made from Portugal’s famous conservas culture.
- And finally, a beautifully prepared octopus dish by a Lisbon chef.

Most of these Portuguese street foods might look humble, but together they reveal how Lisbon eats, lives, and breathes.
If you love traditional food, travel, or planning a trip to Lisbon, I hope this gives you a genuine taste of the city. 

Timestamps:
0:00 - Lisbon Food
0:27 - Bifana at O' Trevo
1:48 - Pastéis de Belém
3:57 - Bola de Berlim
5:00 - Peri Peri Chicken at "Frangasqueira Nacional" 
8:31 - Pão com chouriço
9:54 - Pastéis de Bacalhau
11:45 - Portuguese Octopus at "Time Out Market"
13:06 - Canned Seafood at "Sol & Pesca"

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
This is a travel for food channel and I enjoy eating street food around the world! Eating street food from different countries is what keeps me alive :D

Subscribe to my channel for more food and travel vlogs!  � http://www.youtube.com/user/DanFandelli?sub_confirmation=1
